title: Remanence: open tape archival infrastructure and the RAO object format
description: Remanence is open Rust infrastructure for writing archives to LTO tape and reading them back decades later: library control, tape I/O, erasure-coded parity, and a self-describing on-tape object format. The repository is the reference implementation of two published specifications: the RAO (Rem Archive Object) Format 1.0 — a constrained POSIX pax tar container with per-file SHA-256 identities, closed-form byte-range addressing, a deterministic CBOR manifest, and an HPKE wrapped-DEK encrypted representation — and the REM-PARITY 1.0 tape format for catalog-less, parity-protected recovery from bare tape. The specifications and their pinned test-vector archive are in specs/publication/. Licensing is a permissive split: the Rust reference implementation is Apache-2.0, the specification prose is CC-BY-4.0, and the conformance test vectors are CC0-1.0.
